narrative.headline: 2020 Nissan Rogue: forward collision avoidance tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 147 complaints with NHTSA. Forward collision avoidance is by far the most reported area at 37%, followed by the service brakes (21%) and the electrical system (19%). No deaths are reported. The electrical system stands out for 3 fires, and air bags, though only 5% of reports, account for the most injuries (3), so both are worth a close look.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 54 reports (37%), tied to 4 crashes and 1 injury. The most reported area by a wide margin.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 31 reports (21%), with 1 crash and no injuries. Worth testing the brakes thoroughly on a drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 28 reports (19%), including 3 with fire and 1 injury. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Seats
issues.blurb: 9 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. A smaller, lower-severity complaint area.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 8 reports (5%) but the most injuries of any area: 3 injuries plus 1 crash. Small in volume, serious in outcome.
